title: AlethOS ToolPassport Audit Standard
description: Core dimensions, evidence types, and deterministic finding points for ToolPassport audits.

dimensions.title: Capability Clarity
dimensions.description: Capabilities, limits, and intended uses are explicit and evidence-bound.

dimensions.title: Interface Openness
dimensions.description: Interfaces and integration contracts are documented and inspectable.

dimensions.title: Automation Readiness
dimensions.description: The tool supports stable automated operation, errors, logs, and recovery.

dimensions.title: Data Portability
dimensions.description: Data, configuration, and workflows can be exported or migrated.

dimensions.title: Permission Risk
dimensions.description: Sensitive permissions and side effects are explicit, bounded, and controllable.

dimensions.title: Evidence Quality
dimensions.description: Important claims are supported by reliable, locatable, and reviewable evidence.

dimensions.title: Ecosystem Fit
dimensions.description: The tool is composable, replaceable, maintainable, and suitable for long-running workflows.

evidence_types.title: User Material
evidence_types.description: Material explicitly supplied and authorized by the user.

evidence_types.title: Official Website
evidence_types.description: Public content published on the tool owner's official website.

evidence_types.title: Official Documentation
evidence_types.description: Versioned or current documentation published by the tool owner.

evidence_types.title: GitHub README
evidence_types.description: README content from an identified public source repository revision.

evidence_types.title: GitHub Metadata
evidence_types.description: Repository metadata tied to an identified public repository.

evidence_types.title: Public Example
evidence_types.description: A locatable public example that demonstrates a claimed behavior.

scoring_rules.description: Awards points when evidence supports a positive capability or quality.
Awards points when a risk is explicitly bounded and controlled.
